note: Small, deterministic fallbacks for connecting a current story to official numbers. A reviewed entry in home-editorial.json always takes precedence.

rules.text: If trade friction increases, USD/MXN should rise before monthly export totals weaken. If talks reduce the risk, the sequence should reverse. The peso is the early signal; the next export release is the slower check.
If inflation keeps falling while the policy rate is unchanged, the gap between them widens. That should support the peso first. A renewed rise in inflation would weaken the read.

If the investment story is becoming measurable growth, economic activity and goods exports should improve in later monthly releases. If both stay flat, the announcement has not yet become broad activity.
If tighter US enforcement is materially affecting Mexican workers, remittances should weaken in a later monthly release. A stable remittance series would weaken that read.
